channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Final Extension Announced for Hajj 2026 Second Instalment Payment
item.description: The Ministry of Religious Affairs and Interfaith Harmony has announced a final three-day extension for submission of Hajj 2026 dues, urging all applicants to pay their second instalment by Wednesday. ...

item.title: TMC Emphasises Bengali Identity Ahead of 2026 West Bengal Elections
item.description: As West Bengal prepares for its 2026 assembly elections, the ruling Trinamool Congress (TMC) has adopted a campaign deeply rooted in identity politics. Central to their strategy is the promotion of Be...

item.title: Spotlight Highlights Gallo Music Africa Centenary, Local Film Release and Family Animation
item.description: Spotlight brings South African audiences a digest of the latest in entertainment. This week, the feature focuses on three major highlights. Firstly, the local film 'The Heart Is a Muscle,' written and...

item.title: Byelection for Farrer seat set for May 9 with major parties and independents contesting
item.description: The byelection for the Farrer seat in regional New South Wales, vacated by former opposition leader Sussan Ley, is scheduled for Saturday, 9 May. The Speaker of the House of Representatives announced ...

item.title: Iranian fans highlight oppression of players at Women’s Asian Cup in Australia
item.description: During the opening match of the Women’s Asian Cup at Gold Coast Stadium, Iranian players remained silent during their national anthem, reflecting the severe restrictions and threats imposed on them by...

item.title: Albanese and Canadian PM call for de-escalation amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has moderated his earlier support for US-Israeli strikes on Iran, emphasising the need for broader de-escalation in the Middle East. Speaking alongside Canad...

item.title: Review of 'Subedaar': Anil Kapoor and Radhikka Madan in a Neo-Western Hindi Thriller
item.description: Suresh Triveni’s latest Hindi film 'Subedaar', streaming on Prime Video, unfolds in a fictional Wild West-style town in North India called Kokh, dominated by lawlessness and a single criminal family. ...

item.title: How Iran’s Crisis Could Affect the Red Sea and Horn of Africa
item.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in March 2026, caused by US and Israeli airstrikes in Tehran, marks a significant political change in Iran, but may not drastically alter the country’s regional inf...

item.title: Iran Delays Khamenei's Funeral Amid Escalating US-Israel War; US Senate Rejects Limits on Trump's Military Actions
item.description: The US and Israel war against Iran don reach im sixth day on Thursday, March 5, 2026, with over 1,000 people don die, mostly for Iran side. Iran don postpone the funeral for im late Supreme Leader Aya...

item.title: Insurance Firms in London Stopped Tanker Traffic Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Iran-Israel Tensions
item.description: In March 2026, during heightened Iran-Israel tensions, a group of twelve insurance firms based in London effectively halted all maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. The International Group o...

item.title: Implications of the Iran Conflict for Russia and Ukraine
item.description: The conflict in Iran has significant implications for the ongoing war in Ukraine. While geographically distant, developments in the Middle East are likely to influence Russia’s strategy and global ene...

item.title: Inside the Daily Routine of CBC Morning Live Host David Common
item.description: David Common, the newest host of CBC Morning Live, starts his day extraordinarily early, often rising at 3:38 a.m. before heading to the studio in Toronto. His mornings are tightly scheduled, beginnin...

item.title: Amit Shah launches mascots and digital platforms for Census 2027
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah recently unveiled the mascots for Census 2027, named ‘Pragati’ (female) and ‘Vikas’ (male), representing equal participation of women and men in India's developmental jou...

item.title: Nottingham Killer Valdo Calocane Assaulted Officer Before Fatal Stabbings
item.description: Valdo Calocane, the man responsible for the tragic stabbings in Nottingham in June 2023, had previously attacked a police officer less than two years before the fatal event. The assault occurred in Se...

item.title: Israel and Iran Escalate Military Strikes Across the Middle East
item.description: The conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has intensified, spreading across the Middle East and impacting both military and civilian areas. Israel has carried out multiple airstrikes ag...

item.title: PM Modi Stresses Diplomacy Over Military Action in Iran‑Israel and Ukraine Conflicts
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India has strongly emphasised that military conflict alone cannot resolve disputes, whether in West Asia between Iran and Israel or in Europe in the Russia‑Ukraine war,...

item.title: Congo-Brazzaville Presidential Election 2026: Incumbent Sassou Nguesso Faces Fragmented Opposition
item.description: Congo-Brazzaville is set to hold its presidential election on 15 March 2026. The incumbent president, Denis Sassou Nguesso, who has been in power since 1979, benefits from a strong political majority ...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla says ICC bribery allegations are politically motivated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Ombudsman Jesus Crispin “Boying” Remulla on March 5 stated that the recent allegations by 18 self-proclaimed ex-marines regarding supposed bribery linked to the International Cri...

item.title: TikToker Peller announces nationwide tour across major Nigerian cities
item.description: Peller, the popular TikToker and streamer, don announce say e go dey do nationwide tour starting March 6, 2026. The tour go cover plenty major cities like Benin City, Port Harcourt, Warri, Asaba, Abuj...

item.title: Western Cape High Court Dismisses R17 Million Claim by Wrongfully Detained Man
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has dismissed a R17 million damages claim by Vuyisa Eric Njikelana, who was detained for 19 months on murder charges before being acquitted. The court found that Njikelana ...
